howItWorks.intro: This bonus tax calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Flat method: 22% federal withholding on bonuses under $1M
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Why are bonuses taxed so heavily?
faq.answer: Bonuses are not taxed at a higher rate — they are withheld at a flat 22% federal rate (or 37% over $1M). Your actual tax rate depends on total annual income.

faq.question: Will I get some back at tax time?
faq.answer: If the flat 22% withholding exceeds your effective tax rate, yes — you will get a refund. If your marginal rate is higher than 22%, you may owe more.

faq.question: What is the aggregate method?
faq.answer: Your employer combines your bonus with regular pay for that period and withholds based on the combined amount. This often results in higher withholding.

faq.question: Does FICA apply to bonuses?
faq.answer: Yes. Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) apply to bonuses. Social Security has a wage cap of $168,600 in 2026.
